message:
refactor(core,runtime,render,tty): extract shutdown_signal module, fix set_raw wide-head ownership, fmt cleanup

Extract shutdown_signal into standalone module (ftui-core):
- Move the pending termination signal API (record/query/clear with
  AtomicI32) out of terminal_session.rs into a new top-level
  `shutdown_signal` submodule in ftui-core's lib.rs. The signal state is
  process-wide and consumed by both the runtime event loop and multiple
  backend crates (ftui-tty, terminal_session). Keeping it in
  terminal_session created a false coupling: backends had to import
  terminal session internals just to set a global flag, and the runtime
  had to reach into terminal_session to check it. The new module makes
  the dependency graph honest — signal state is a thin, standalone
  concern with no knowledge of sessions, I/O, or cleanup logic.
- Remove the now-unused AtomicI32 import and PENDING_TERMINATION_SIGNAL
  static from terminal_session.rs; internal callers (wait_for_shutdown_ack,
  SignalGuard) now route through crate::shutdown_signal::.

Update all callsites across crates:
- ftui-runtime program.rs: replace all ftui_core::terminal_session::
  pending/record/clear calls with the new path. Add local
  check_termination_signal() and clear_termination_signal() thin wrappers
  so the run-loop reads cleanly without repeating the full module path on
  every poll point. Remove the #[cfg(feature = "crossterm-compat")] gate
  from the post-loop clear call — the shutdown_signal module is
  unconditionally compiled, so headless/wasm builds that never install a
  signal handler simply see None and the clear is a harmless no-op. This
  eliminates a subtle bug where non-crossterm builds would skip clearing
  the flag, potentially leaking a stale signal across test runs.
- ftui-tty lib.rs: update the signal hook to use
  ftui_core::shutdown_signal:: for both record and poll, matching the new
  module boundary.
- Remove unused `std::error::Error as _` import from program.rs.

Fix set_raw() wide-head ownership semantics (ftui-render buffer.rs):
- Previously, set_raw() unconditionally ran cleanup_overlap() and
  cleanup_orphaned_tails() even when the caller was writing a wide-head
  cell. This was wrong for the intended use case: callers who build wide
  glyphs manually (write head, then write continuation) had their
  continuation cells immediately cleared by the orphan-tail cleanup that
  fires after the head write. The fix detects raw wide-head writes
  (width > 1 and not a continuation cell) and skips both cleanup_overlap
  and cleanup_orphaned_tails for those writes, leaving continuation
  ownership entirely in the caller's hands. Single-width and continuation
  writes still get full cleanup as before.
- Add test set_raw_wide_head_preserves_manual_tail_cells verifying that
  replacing a wide head via set_raw does not clobber the adjacent
  continuation cell, and that the dirty span is limited to column 0 only.

Formatting normalization (generic_diff.rs, generic_repr.rs, mode_typestate.rs):
- Apply rustfmt conventions: collapse short Product::patch() and
  enable_bracketed_paste/enable_mouse/enable_focus_events signatures
  from multi-line to single-line where they fit comfortably within the
  line limit.
- Remove Rust's `1 .0` tuple-of-tuple field access spacing artifacts
  (e.g. `repr.1 .0` → `repr.1.0`, `v.value.1 .1 .0` → `v.value.1.1.0`).
  These spaces were inserted by older rustfmt to disambiguate from float
  literals but are no longer needed and hurt readability.
- Reformat long Sum<...> type signatures in generic_repr test module to
  wrap at nested levels instead of running past the line limit.

Beads issue tracker: update issue status and comments.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"bd-1lg.8","title":"Galaxy-brain decision card widget","description":"Standalone reusable widget showing progressive-disclosure decision transparency. Level 0: traffic light (green/yellow/red). Level 1: plain English explanation. Level 2: evidence terms and posterior probabilities. Level 3: full Bayesian factor breakdown. Used for diff strategy, frame budget, and any Bayesian decision. This is the adoption wedge widget -- embeddable in any terminal app. TRACING: span 'galaxybrain.render' with fields: disclosure_level, decision_type, confidence. Feature-gated via ftui-runtime optional dependency to keep default widget crate lightweight.","created_at":"2026-03-05T20:47:54Z"},{"id":1031,"issue_id":"bd-1lg.8","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Removed galaxy-brain feature flag — decision_card module and DecisionCard widget are now unconditionally enabled. ftui-runtime is a required dependency. Assert no panics on degenerate inputs (zero-area, massive area).","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:01:12.829378760Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-14T21:47:33.380132221Z","closed_at":"2026-02-14T21:47:33.380113146Z","close_reason":"Completed: Added 24 property-based tests across two new test files covering non-overlapping rects, extended constraint types (FitContent/FitContentBounded/FitMin), rect ordering, contiguous positioning, measurer determinism, SizeConstraints invariants, and degenerate input resilience.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["layout","ring3","test","unit"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1lg.9","depends_on_id":"bd-1lg","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-05T16:31:30Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1lg.9","depends_on_id":"bd-1lg.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-05T16:31:30Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-1lgz8","title":"epic(graveyard): Killer Demo — Galaxy-Brain Transparency Visualization","description":"## Summary

Live UI demonstrating the Bayesian diff strategy selector's decision-making in real time. Shows posterior distribution, loss matrix, chosen strategy, confidence level, and automatic fallback under drift. Must be CI-executable as a FrankenLab scenario. The 'killer demo' from FrankenSuite summary. This is what makes FrankenTUI feel like an alien artifact -- the math is visible and beautiful.

## Graveyard Methodology

The galaxy-brain transparency demo is the flagship "show, don't tell" artifact for the graveyard methodology. It demonstrates that FrankenTUI doesn't just USE alien CS concepts -- it makes their operation VISIBLE and AUDITABLE.

## Detail

The demo displays:
1. **Posterior distribution**: Bar chart showing probability of each diff strategy being optimal
2. **Loss matrix**: Heatmap of expected loss for each (strategy, regime) pair
3. **Current strategy**: Highlighted with confidence level and justification
4. **Evidence stream**: Scrolling log of observations feeding the Bayesian update
5. **Regime indicator**: Current detected regime (stable/bursty/resize/degraded)
6. **Top evidence terms**: With Bayes factors for each active decision
7. **Confidence intervals**: For all active decisions

Interactive features:
- Force drift: inject anomalous observations to trigger regime change
- Override strategy: manually select a strategy, see posterior update
- Time travel: replay past decisions from EvidenceLedger
- Disclosure levels: L0 (summary) -> L1 (posterior) -> L2 (loss matrix) -> L3 (full evidence)

FrankenLab integration:
- Scenario file defines the demo sequence
- Deterministic replay from EvidenceLedger data
- Screenshot checkpoints for golden frame verification
- CI-runnable: demo executes headlessly and verifies output

## Acceptance Criteria

1. All 7 display elements rendering correctly
2. Interactive features working (drift injection, override, time travel)
3. 4-level disclosure (L0-L3) implemented
4. FrankenLab scenario file for CI execution
5. Golden frame checksums for demo checkpoints
6. Demo renders at 60fps even with full L3 disclosure
7. All 7 decision points from Evidence Ledger are represented","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-02-13T09:26:50.892151876Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-13T23:09:18.000267524Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["demo","frankenlab","galaxy-brain","graveyard","transparency"]}
-{"id":"bd-1lgz8.1","title":"task(galaxy-brain): Build decision card widget (Levels 0-3 disclosure)","description":"## Summary

Build the galaxy-brain decision card widget that displays the Bayesian diff strategy selector's current state with progressive disclosure from L0 (one-line summary) to L3 (full posterior + loss matrix + evidence stream).

## Detail

Each card renders a single Bayesian decision as: equation (Unicode math), substituted values, one-line English intuition, evidence bar chart.

Disclosure levels:

**Level 0 — Summary line (outcome only)**:
- "Diff: incremental (95% confident, stable regime)"
- Single line, suitable for status bar
- Color-coded: green (>90%), yellow (70-90%), red (<70%)

**Level 1 — Top factors (posterior bars)**:
- Horizontal bar chart: one bar per candidate strategy
- Bar width = posterior probability
- Active strategy highlighted
- Confidence interval shown

**Level 2 — Full posterior (loss matrix)**:
- Heatmap: rows = strategies, columns = possible true states
- Cell color = expected loss (green -> red)
- Optimal strategy highlighted per column
- Minimax strategy indicated

**Level 3 — Equations + proofs (full evidence)**:
- Scrolling log of recent observations
- Each observation: timestamp, metric name, value, contribution to posterior update
- Bayesian update visualization: prior -> likelihood -> posterior for each observation
- Historical trend: posterior over last N frames as sparkline

Widget properties:
- Responsive: adapts to available space (collapses levels when squeezed)
- Themeable: respects FrankenTUI color scheme
- Animatable: transitions between levels use damped spring physics
- Keyboard navigable: expand/collapse with arrow keys

## Acceptance Criteria

1. All 4 disclosure levels implemented and rendering correctly
2. Responsive layout: graceful degradation when space is limited
3. Animation between levels using existing damped spring physics
4. Keyboard navigation: arrow keys expand/collapse, Tab cycles strategies
5. Unit tests for each level's rendering
6. Golden frame checksums for each level
7. Evidence bar chart rendering for each decision","status":"in_progress","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-13T09:27:04.056671632Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-05T22:03:01.313500782Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["demo","disclosure","galaxy-brain","graveyard","widget"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1lgz8.1","depends_on_id":"bd-1lgz8","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-05T16:31:30Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1lgz8.1","depends_on_id":"bd-3jlw5.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-05T16:31:30Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1lgz8.1","depends_on_id":"bd-6i8zb","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-05T16:31:30Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
-{"id":"bd-1lgz8.2","title":"task(galaxy-brain): Implement drift-triggered fallback visualization","description":"## Summary

Visualize the automatic fallback mechanism when the Bayesian diff strategy selector detects regime drift. Show the confidence dropping, the fallback trigger point, and the transition to the deterministic safety policy. Includes live posterior sparkline showing posterior probability evolution over last N frames for each decision domain.

## Detail

### Live Posterior Sparkline
Real-time sparkline showing posterior probability evolution over last N frames for each decision domain. Color-coded: green=confident, yellow=uncertain, red=fallback zone. Click to expand into full decision card.

### Drift Visualization Flow
1. Normal operation: posterior is concentrated, confidence is high (green)
2. Drift onset: anomalous observations arrive, posterior broadens (yellow)
3. Confidence threshold: confidence drops below 0.7, border turns red
4. Fallback trigger: automatic switch to deterministic policy, flash animation
5. Recovery: new observations stabilize posterior, return to normal (green fade-in)

### Visual Elements
- Confidence sparkline: last 60 frames of confidence values
- Drift indicator: vertical line on sparkline at fallback trigger point
- Regime banner: current regime displayed prominently during transition
- Strategy transition: animated swap from Bayesian -> deterministic and back
- Evidence burst: rapid log entries during drift shown as accelerated scroll

### Interactive Drift Injection
- Button/hotkey to inject anomalous observations (simulated scroll burst, resize, etc.)
- Slider to control drift intensity (0 = no drift, 1 = maximum anomaly)
- Timeline scrubber to replay past drift events from EvidenceLedger

## Acceptance Criteria

1. Live posterior sparkline renders per-domain color-coded probability evolution
2. Drift visualization correctly shows confidence decline and recovery
3. Fallback trigger is visually prominent (animation, color change)
4. Interactive drift injection works with configurable intensity
5. Timeline scrubber replays past events accurately
6. 60fps maintained during drift visualization (no ironic frame drops during perf demo)
7. Golden frame checksums for drift sequence checkpoints
8. Click-to-expand from sparkline to full decision card","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-13T09:27:16.948582155Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-13T23:09:00.538517711Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["demo","drift","fallback","galaxy-brain","graveyard","visualization"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1lgz8.2","depends_on_id":"bd-1lgz8","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-05T16:31:30Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1lgz8.2","depends_on_id":"bd-1lgz8.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-05T16:31:30Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1lgz8.2","depends_on_id":"bd-3jlw5.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-05T16:31:30Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

 {"id":"bd-1lgz8.3","title":"task(galaxy-brain): Create FrankenLab scenario file for demo","description":"## Summary

Create a FrankenLab scenario file that scripts the entire galaxy-brain transparency demo for automated CI execution. The scenario defines the input sequence, checkpoint frames, and expected golden frame hashes.

## Detail

FrankenLab scenario structure:
```toml
[scenario]
name = "galaxy-brain-transparency-demo"
description = "Demonstrates Bayesian diff strategy transparency with drift injection"
terminal_size = { width = 120, height = 40 }
duration_frames = 600  # 10 seconds at 60fps

[[checkpoints]]
frame = 0
description = "Initial state: L0 disclosure, stable regime"
golden_hash = "..."

[[checkpoints]]
frame = 60
description = "Expand to L1: posterior bars visible"
golden_hash = "..."

[[events]]
frame = 0
type = "keypress"
key = "Enter"  # Open demo

[[events]]
frame = 30
type = "keypress"
key = "Right"  # Expand to L1

[[events]]
frame = 120
type = "inject_drift"
intensity = 0.8  # Trigger drift

# ... etc.
```

Scenario phases:
1. Frames 0-60: Show L0, expand to L1
2. Frames 60-120: Expand to L2, show loss matrix
3. Frames 120-240: Inject drift, watch confidence decline, fallback trigger
4. Frames 240-360: Recovery, posterior re-concentrates
5. Frames 360-480: Expand to L3, show full evidence
6. Frames 480-600: Return to L0, final state verification

## Acceptance Criteria

1. Scenario file defines complete demo sequence
2. All checkpoints have golden frame hashes
3. CI runs scenario headlessly and verifies all checkpoints
4. Scenario completes in <30 seconds on CI runner
5. Failure output shows which checkpoint failed with visual diff
6. Scenario can be run interactively for manual demo","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-13T09:27:29.335325266Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-13T09:27:35.543349891Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["ci","demo","frankenlab","graveyard","scenario"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1lgz8.3","depends_on_id":"bd-1lgz8","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-05T16:31:30Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1lgz8.3","depends_on_id":"bd-1lgz8.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-05T16:31:30Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1lgz8.3","depends_on_id":"bd-1lgz8.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-05T16:31:30Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-1lzl8","title":"Samples: tagging taxonomy + naming conventions","description":"Goal
- Define a consistent tagging taxonomy and naming conventions for Mermaid samples.

Deliverables
- Tag categories (type, complexity, stress, unicode, labels, nested, dense).
- Naming convention for sample IDs and display names.
- Validation rules enforced by loader or tests.

Acceptance
- Samples follow consistent naming/tagging; filter/search behaves predictably.
","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"subtask","created_at":"2026-02-05T15:34:48.123812192Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-05T18:43:14.347080515Z","closed_at":"2026-02-05T18:43:14.347062992Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["demo-showcase","mermaid","samples","subtask"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1lzl8","depends_on_id":"bd-2sp01","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-05T16:31:30Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":172,"issue_id":"bd-1lzl8","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"GoldBear claiming. Will define the tagging taxonomy and naming conventions for Mermaid samples based on existing sample metadata schema.","created_at":"2026-02-05T18:38:04Z"},{"id":173,"issue_id":"bd-1lzl8","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"GoldBear: Implementation complete. Added:
1. KNOWN_TAGS constant - 33 tags organized into 6 semantic categories (Structure, Content, Relationships, Density, Rendering, Fallback) with doc comments explaining the taxonomy
2. KNOWN_EDGE_CASE_TAGS constant - 13 edge-case tags for regression coverage tracking
3. 9 new validation tests:
   - all_sample_tags_are_known: validates tags field against KNOWN_TAGS
   - all_sample_edge_cases_are_known: validates edge_cases against KNOWN_EDGE_CASE_TAGS
   - known_tags_are_sorted_within_categories: no duplicates in any tag list
   - sample_ids_are_kebab_case: IDs must be lowercase-dash format
   - sample_ids_start_with_family_prefix: IDs start with family name (skip unsupported)
   - sample_names_are_title_case: names must be Title Case
   - each_sample_has_at_least_one_tag: no empty tags
   - each_sample_has_at_least_one_feature: no empty features
   - known_tags_all_exercised: at most 5 unused tags allowed

All 165 mermaid_showcase tests pass, clippy clean.","created_at":"2026-02-05T18:43:09Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-1m0wa","title":"frankenterm-web: stale diff_to_patches fallback","description":"When a BufferDiff is computed for an old geometry and later applied to a resized Buffer, diff coordinates can be out of bounds. Add guard in diff_to_patches to fall back to full_buffer_patch, plus regression test.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","assignee":"NavyDesert","created_at":"2026-02-10T01:38:49.031696207Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-10T01:42:06.512742545Z","closed_at":"2026-02-10T01:42:06.512723159Z","close_reason":"Merged in 786730ae; adds stale diff guard + regression test","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

 {"id":"bd-382tk","title":"epic(recipe-F): Policy-as-Data Controllers (Composition Recipe)","description":"Graveyard Composition Recipe F: Safe adaptive behavior shipping. Primitives: Decision (S0.4) + Evidence (S0.19) + Sequential Tests (S0.18) + Proof Artifacts (S0.7). Package diff strategy, resize coalescing, degradation policies as signed, sandboxed, updateable data artifacts. Progressive delivery: shadow -> canary -> ramp -> default. Any verification failure triggers fallback to baked-in deterministic policy.

**User Value:** Lets advanced users swap and tune adaptive policies without recompiling, enabling domain-specific optimization.

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- Policy definitions loadable from configuration
- Hot-swap between policies without restart
- Evidence ledger tracks policy switches
- Default policy equivalent to current hardcoded behavior
- All existing tests pass","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-02-13T21:50:34.060964993Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-13T23:09:41.356897755Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["alien","graveyard","policy","recipe"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-382tk","depends_on_id":"bd-3rss7","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-05T16:31:32Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-382tk","depends_on_id":"bd-fp38v","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-05T16:31:32Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-382tk.1","title":"task(e2e): E2E test suite for Recipe F Policy-as-Data","description":"## E2E Integration Test: Recipe F Policy-as-Data Controllers (EV=recipe)

### Scope
Full integration test loading policy artifact, shadow-running against baked-in policy, logging divergences, and verifying fallback on verification failure.

### Test Scenario
1. Create a policy artifact bundle containing:
   - Diff strategy policy (thresholds, weights)
   - Resize coalescing policy (delay, max-batch)
   - Degradation policy (level thresholds, hysteresis)
2. Sign the policy artifact with test key.
3. Load policy artifact into FrankenTUI runtime.
4. Shadow-run: execute both loaded policy and baked-in deterministic policy in parallel.
5. Log every decision where loaded policy diverges from baked-in.
6. Progressive delivery test:
   a. Shadow mode (100% baked-in, 0% loaded) - just log divergences.
   b. Canary mode (90% baked-in, 10% loaded) - verify canary decisions.
   c. Ramp mode (50/50) - verify no regression.
   d. Default mode (100% loaded) - full switch.
7. Inject verification failure: corrupt policy artifact signature.
8. Verify immediate fallback to baked-in deterministic policy.
9. Verify fallback is seamless (no dropped frames, no visual glitch).

### JSONL Structured Logging Specification
Each policy decision emits:
```json
{
  "event": "recipe_f_policy",
  "ts_ns": u64,
  "frame_id": u64,
  "policy_source": "loaded|baked_in|shadow",
  "delivery_stage": "shadow|canary|ramp|default",
  "decision_point": "diff_strategy|resize_coalesce|degradation",
  "loaded_action": "string",
  "baked_in_action": "string",
  "divergence": bool,
  "divergence_reason": "string|null",
  "policy_version": "string",
  "signature_valid": bool,
  "fallback_triggered": bool,
  "frame_time_ms": f64
}
```

### Success Criteria
- Shadow mode logs all divergences without affecting rendering.
- Progressive delivery stages execute in correct order.
- Signature verification failure triggers immediate fallback (within 1 frame).
- fallback_triggered == true when signature_valid == false.
- No dropped frames during fallback transition.
- Loaded policy produces same or better frame times than baked-in.
- No panics, no unsafe code (#\![forbid(unsafe_code)]).
- JSONL log is schema-compliant and parseable.
- Policy artifact is a self-contained data file (no executable code).","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-13T22:26:39.822420841Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-13T23:09:34.451077083Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["e2e","logging","test"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-382tk.1","depends_on_id":"bd-382tk","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-05T16:31:32Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-382tk.1","depends_on_id":"bd-382tk.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-05T16:31:32Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
